draft.introduction: Mary and Martha: Martha is distracted by serving while Mary sits at Jesus’ feet. Jesus’ declaration that Mary “has chosen the good portion” became a foundational text for the contemplative tradition.

translation.text: Now as they went on their way, Jesus entered a village. And a woman named Martha welcomed him into her house. And she had a sister called Mary, who sat at the Lord’s feet and listened to his teaching. But Martha was distracted with much serving. And she went up to him and said, “Lord, do you not care that my sister has left me to serve alone?” But the Lord answered her, “Martha, Martha, you are anxious and troubled about many things, but one thing is necessary. Mary has chosen the good portion, which will not be taken away from her.”

translator_notes.note: The “good portion” (ἀγαθὴν μερίδα) became the classic proof-text for the superiority of the contemplative life over the active life in both Eastern and Western monasticism. Origen, Augustine, and Meister Eckhart all built on this passage.
